items.q: What does the time signature 6/8 indicate?
items.correct: Two beats per bar, each beat divided into three (compound duple).
items.misconception: Six beats per bar, all equal in stress.
items.why: Students count 6/8 as six equal beats. In practice it's felt as TWO dotted-crotchet beats subdivided into three quavers each - compound duple, not simple sextuple.

items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: Students conflate modulation with any musical change. Specifically, modulation is a KEY CHANGE, usually established by a perfect cadence in the new key - a structural device.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: Which 1969 Miles Davis album is foundational jazz fusion?
items.why: Correct answer: Bitches Brew. “Kind of Blue” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1986 Paul Simon album featured South African musicians?
items.why: Correct answer: Graceland. “Hearts and Bones” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Peter Gabriel found WOMAD?
items.why: The real figure is 1982 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
